title: Let's Go! Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To go somewhere on your own two feet.
rounds.explanation: walk: To go somewhere on your own two feet.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To make a car or bus go where you want.
rounds.explanation: drive: To make a car or bus go where you want.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Moving very quickly, so you get there in no time.
rounds.explanation: fast: Moving very quickly, so you get there in no time.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A long vehicle that carries many people at once.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Parts that wipe rain off a car window.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Moving gently without hurrying, so you take a long time.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We walk to school every morning.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The plane flies above the clouds.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Dad will drive us to school.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence tells where a car goes?
rounds.explanation: Cars drive on roads; the sea is for boats, the sky is for planes and the rails are for trains.

rounds.prompt: Which word tells you that something moves very quickly?
rounds.explanation: Fast means quick; slow is the opposite, heavy tells weight and quiet tells sound.

rounds.prompt: What should you do before you cross the road?
rounds.explanation: Stopping, looking and listening keeps you safe; the other choices are all dangerous.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What two colours was the big bus?
choices: She called out to her, and Amal ran and hugged her
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Yellow and red

rounds.prompt: How did Amal travel first, before she went by bus?
choices: I go by ___. (for example: I go by bus.)
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She walked

rounds.prompt: How do we know Grandmother was glad to see Amal?
choices: It goes on the ___. (for example: on the road.)
She called out to her, and Amal ran and hugged her
rounds.answer: She called out to her, and Amal ran and hugged her
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She called out to her, and Amal ran and hugged her

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
rounds.target: Point to transport pictures as an adult names them, then say your own.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
rounds.target: and answer about a busy street picture.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Role-play
rounds.target: Role-play a bus ride with your friends.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Please sit down on your seat.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
Put this unit sentence in order.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'float' mean in this unit?
choices: To stay on top of water without sinking.
To go somewhere on your own two feet.
A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.
rounds.answer: To stay on top of water without sinking.
rounds.explanation: float: To stay on top of water without sinking.

rounds.prompt: What does 'go' mean in this unit?
choices: To go somewhere on your own two feet.
A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.
To start moving or leave for another place.
rounds.answer: To start moving or leave for another place.
rounds.explanation: go: To start moving or leave for another place.

rounds.prompt: What does 'bicycle' mean in this unit?
choices: A vehicle with wings that flies high in the sky.
A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.
To go somewhere on your own two feet.
rounds.answer: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.
rounds.explanation: bicycle: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'town'?
rounds.explanation: town is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'red'?
rounds.explanation: red is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'seat'?
rounds.explanation: seat is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A vehicle with four wheels that a family rides in.
A vehicle with wings that flies high in the sky.
Big sheets of cloth that catch wind and move a boat.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: To move through the air, like a bird or a plane.
To put your body onto a chair or the floor.
A long hard path where cars and buses travel.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: To finish moving and stay still in one place.
Going a big distance from one end to the other.
The colour of the sky and the deep sea.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which one has only two wheels?
rounds.explanation: A bicycle has two wheels; cars, buses and trains all have many more.

rounds.prompt: Which one moves along rails?
rounds.explanation: A train runs on rails; a car uses the road, a boat uses water and a plane uses the sky.

rounds.prompt: Musa comes to school in a bus. Which sentence is correct?
rounds.explanation: We say I go by bus for one bus; the other sentences have the words in the wrong order or the wrong form.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word names a way to travel?
rounds.explanation: You can travel in a bus; a window, an apple and a cloud do not take you anywhere.

rounds.prompt: Which one travels on water?
rounds.explanation: A boat goes on the water; a bus and a bicycle go on the road and a train goes on rails.

rounds.prompt: Which one flies in the sky?
rounds.explanation: A plane flies; a car and a train stay on the ground and a boat stays on the water.
